skill.name: SEO Structure Architect
skill.description: Analyzes and optimizes content structure including header hierarchy, suggests schema markup, and internal linking opportunities. Creates search-friendly content organization. Use PROACTIVELY for content structuring.
skill.summary: Optimizes content structure for search engines with header hierarchy, schema markup, and internal linking recommendations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only markdown guidance. No executable code, no network requests, no file system access, and no external commands. The skill provides SEO best practices for content structure and is safe for publication.

content.value_statement: Poor content structure hurts search rankings. This skill analyzes header hierarchies, recommends schema markup, and identifies internal linking opportunities to improve SEO performance.
actual_capabilities: Analyze and optimize header tag hierarchy (H1-H6) for logical flow
Recommend appropriate schema markup types (Article, FAQ, HowTo, Review)
Identify internal linking opportunities and silo structure
Create table of contents and content depth assessments
Provide JSON-LD schema code snippets for implementation
Optimize content for featured snippet eligibility
limitations: Cannot access live search engine data or rankings
Does not analyze backlink profiles or domain authority
Cannot test schema markup validity (only provides recommendations)
Does not replace manual SEO audits for complex sites

use_cases.title: Blog Post Optimization
use_cases.description: Structure a new blog post with proper header hierarchy and schema markup to improve search visibility and featured snippet chances.

use_cases.title: Website Content Audit
use_cases.description: Review existing website content for structural improvements, internal linking gaps, and schema implementation opportunities.

use_cases.title: E-commerce Product Pages
use_cases.description: Optimize product pages with Review schema, proper headers, and internal linking to category pages for better organic traffic.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Header Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the header structure of my content. I have an article about [TOPIC]. Please review my H1-H6 hierarchy and suggest improvements for SEO.

prompt_templates.title: Schema Markup Request
prompt_templates.prompt: I need schema markup recommendations for my [CONTENT TYPE] page about [TOPIC]. What schema types should I implement and provide JSON-LD code?

prompt_templates.title: Internal Linking Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an internal linking strategy for my [INDUSTRY] website. I have these main pages: [LIST PAGES]. How should I link them for optimal SEO?

prompt_templates.title: Complete Content Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: I am creating a comprehensive guide about [TOPIC]. Please provide: 1) Header hierarchy outline 2) Recommended schema types 3) Internal linking strategy 4) Featured snippet optimization tips

output_examples.input: Analyze my article about 'Best Running Shoes for Beginners' and suggest improvements
output_examples.output: Header Structure: H1: Best Running Shoes for Beginners (primary keyword), H2: Types of Running Shoes (secondary), H2: How to Choose the Right Fit (tertiary), H3: Pronation Guide. Schema: Use Product schema with AggregateRating. Internal links: Link to 'Running Tips' category and 'Best Running Apps' article.

output_examples.input: What schema should I add to my FAQ page about remote work?
output_examples.output: Implement FAQPage schema with structured Q&A blocks. This enables rich results in search. Also consider adding BreadcrumbList schema for navigation hierarchy.

output_examples.input: Create a silo structure for my digital marketing blog
output_examples.output: Topic Cluster: SEO > Technical SEO, Local SEO, SEO Tools. Topic Cluster: Content Marketing > Blogging, Copywriting, Content Strategy. Cross-link clusters only via main pillar pages. Each cluster needs parent topic page with child articles.

best_practices: Use only one H1 per page matching the primary keyword
Maintain logical hierarchy without skipping header levels
Include target keywords naturally in headers without keyword stuffing
anti_patterns: Using multiple H1 tags on a single page
Skipping header levels (H1 directly to H4)
Stuffing keywords into every header tag

faq.question: What is the most important header tag for SEO?
faq.answer: The H1 tag is most important as it tells search engines the main topic. Use exactly one H1 per page with your primary keyword.

faq.question: Does schema markup directly improve rankings?
faq.answer: Schema markup does not directly improve rankings but enables rich results (rich snippets) which increase click-through rates.

faq.question: How many internal links should a page have?
faq.answer: Include 3-5 relevant internal links to related content. Focus on quality and relevance over quantity.

faq.question: Can I use the same keyword in multiple headers?
faq.answer: Use variations and related terms in subheaders. Exact keyword repetition in every header appears unnatural to search engines.

faq.question: What schema types are most valuable for blogs?
faq.answer: Article, FAQPage, BreadcrumbList, and optional HowTo schemas provide the most value for blog content.

faq.question: How do I check if my schema is working?
faq.answer: Use Google's Rich Results Test tool to validate schema markup implementation and see which rich results your page qualifies for.
